description: §41.16.6 — a struct field whose declared type has no v1.0 display mapping (array types among them) with no explicit slot override fires E-TABLEFOR-NO-DISPLAY-MAPPING. `Item` has `{ id, tags: string[] }`; `tags` has no slot. Unmappable shapes include function/snippet/Promise/foreign-code/deep-reactive-proxy types, arrays, and arbitrary opaque types. (Incidental W-PROGRAM-001 + W-STDLIB-SEED-FAILCLOSED co-fire, as in the sibling table-for cases; superset match.)
rationale: §41.16.6 E-TABLEFOR-NO-DISPLAY-MAPPING: a field whose declared type has no v1.0 display mapping, with no explicit `<column field="X">` slot override, has no default rendering. Resolution: exclude the field via `omit=["tags"]`, OR provide an explicit slot with adopter-authored rendering, OR refactor the struct to use a renderable type.
